scheme_name: Special Scheme for Increasing the Storage Capacity of Perishable Products
page_title: Special Scheme for Increasing the Storage Capacity of Perishable Products

details: The scheme “Special Scheme for Increasing the Storage Capacity of Perishable Products” launched by the Horticulture and Food Processing Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to increase cold storage capacity through providing 50% grant assistance to all eligible farmers.
benefits: Grant Assistance: A grant of 50% of the project cost up to a maximum of ₹2,00,00,000/- for cold storage facilities up to 5,000 metric tons capacity.
eligibility: The applicant must be a farmer.
The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

offline: The applicant should contact the District Office of the Horticulture Department.

faqs.question: What is the objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to enhance the storage capacity of perishable products by providing financial support for cold storage facilities.

faqs.question: Which department implements this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Horticulture and Food Processing Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: All farmers are eligible to apply under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The maximum financial assistance is 50% of the project cost, up to ₹2,00,00,000/-.

faqs.question: What is the capacity limit for cold storage under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers cold storage facilities up to 5,000 metric tons capacity.

faqs.question: How can a farmer apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The farmer can apply by contacting the District Office of the Horticulture Department.

faqs.question: Is the assistance provided as a one-time grant or recurring support?
faqs.answer: The assistance is provided as a one-time grant.

faqs.question: Where should the applicant contact for further information or assistance?
faqs.answer: The applicant should contact the District Office of the Horticulture Department, Madhya Pradesh.
